records.ety: [AS. æfter after, behind; akin to Goth. aftaro, aftra, backwards, Icel. aptr, Sw. and Dan. efter, OHG. aftar behind, Dutch and LG. achter, Gr. 'apwte`rw further off. The ending -ter is an old comparative suffix, in E. generally -ther (as in other), and after is a compar. of of, off. √194. See Of; cf. Aft.]

defs: Next; later in time; subsequent; succeeding; as, an after period of life.
notes: ☞ In this sense the word is sometimes needlessly combined with the following noun, by means of a hyphen, as, after-ages, after-act, after-days, after-life. For the most part the words are properly kept separate when after has this meaning.

defs: To ward the stern of the ship; -- applied to any object in the rear part of a vessel; as the after cabin, after hatchway.
notes: It is often combined with its noun; as, after-bowlines, after-braces, after-sails, after-yards, those on the mainmasts and mizzenmasts.

defs: Behind in place; as, men in line one after another.
Below in rank; next to in order.
quotes: Codrus after Phœbus sings the best.

defs: Later in time; subsequent; as, after supper, after three days. It often precedes a clause. Formerly that was interposed between it and the clause.
quotes: After I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

defs: Subsequent to and in consequence of; as, after what you have said, I shall be careful.
Subsequent to and notwithstanding; as, after all our advice, you took that course.
Moving toward from behind; following, in search of; in pursuit of.
quotes: Ye shall not go after other gods.
After whom is the king of Israel come out?

defs: Denoting the aim or object; concerning; in relation to; as, to look after workmen; to inquire after a friend; to thirst after righteousness.
In imitation of; in conformity with; after the manner of; as, to make a thing after a model; a picture after Rubens; the boy takes after his father.
quotes: Our eldest son was named George after his uncle.

defs: According to; in accordance with; in conformity with the nature of; as, he acted after his kind.
quotes: He shall not judge after the sight of his eyes.
They that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh.

defs: According to the direction and influence of; in proportion to; befitting.
quotes: He takes greatness of kingdoms according to bulk and currency, and not after their intrinsic value.

defs: Subsequently in time or place; behind; afterward; as, he follows after.
quotes: It was about the space of three hours after.
notes: ☞ After is prefixed to many words, forming compounds, but retaining its usual signification. The prefix may be adverbial, prepositional, or adjectival; as in after- described, after-dinner, after-part. The hyphen is sometimes needlessly used to connect the adjective after with its noun. See Note under After, a., 1.
